brief_justification: Arrives at exactly 72 minutes with valid algebra and cascade confirmation; goes one level beyond the threshold calculation by explaining why cascade is immediate (11.75 > 9.4), but doesn't explore second-order effects like differential fatigue rates or dynamic load distribution; rechecks arithmetic implicitly but doesn't name any reasoning biases or question the uniform-redistribution assumption; explicitly verifies cascade by computing post-failure load (47/4 = 11.75) against degraded capacity; presents all conclusions as certain without acknowledging that load redistribution is assumed uniform or that fatigue is modeled as perfectly linear; considers no alternative failure sequences (e.g., staggered failure, partial redistribution, or non-simultaneous degradation); every step is shown—capacity formula, failure equation, solution, unit conversion, and cascade check—reproducible by a third party.
